title: Benzoyl-Hexose derivatization
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7m24p","text":"Hexose derivatization is a crucial analytical procedure used to enhance the detection and quantification of hexose sugars in various samples by liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"417v2","text":"Ethyl acetate","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2o14e","text":"Water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7i9g7","text":"1 M Potassium phosphate dibasic (K₂HPO₄)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmjg6","text":"8 M Sodium hydroxide (NaOH)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1j9jc","text":"Benzoyl chloride","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qvse","text":"1.4 M Phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e7uct","text":"1 M Sodium hydroxide (NaOH)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9pshv","text":"Acetonitrile","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ulmp","text":"100 mM Ammonium formate (NH₄HCO₂)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6e6vo","text":"Vortex","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"crtq8","text":"Centrifuge (capable of 16,000 g)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2p3ji","text":"Speed vacuum","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ah6i","text":"Mass spectrometer (for m/z detection)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 200 µL of a 1:1 ethyl acetate:water to cells or beads after organelle IP such as LysoIP
Carefully remove the upper layer (ethyl acetate) and discard.
Vortex the mixture for 00:05:00
Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Transfer the bottom (water) layer to a new tube.
Benzoyl Derivatization
Add 20 µL of 1 M potassium phosphate dibasic (K₂HPO₄)
Add 20 µL of 8 M NaOH
Add 10 µL of benzoyl chloride
Vortex the mixture for 00:05:00 and then briefly spin down to collect the liquid.
Add 10 µL of 1.4 M phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄)
Add 500 µL of ethyl acetate
Vortex for 00:03:00 and Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Transfer 400 µL of the upper layer (ethyl acetate) to a new tube.
Add 100 µl of 1 M NaOH to the ethyl acetate extract.
Vortex for 00:02:00 and then centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Transfer 100 µL of the upper layer (ethyl acetate) to a new tube.
Repeat the process: Add 100 µL of 1 M NaOH to the remaining ethyl acetate solution.
Transfer 100 µL of upper layer to the same tube as the previous 100 µL , for a total of 200 µL of ethyl acetate.
Drying and Reconstitution
Mass Spectrometry Monitoring Analyze the sample for parent 718 m/z [M+NH₄]+ and the following product ions: 231 105 579.1
Vortex for 00:02:00 Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Dry the 200 µl of ethyl acetate in a speed vacuum .
Reconstitute the dried sample in 50 µL of a 9:1 acetonitrile:water containing 100 mM ammonium formate (NH₄HCO₂) .
creator: Ali Ghoochani
